name: contextifly
description: Drop or paste UI screenshots into VS Code — Contextifly turns them into structured developer markdown.

contextifly.backendUrl.description: Base URL of the Contextifly backend (e.g. http://localhost:3000).

contextifly.timeoutMs.description: Maximum time (ms) to wait for a screenshot analysis to complete.

enumDescriptions: Use the backend's configured LLM (no key sent).
Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (OpenRouter, Together, Groq, vLLM, LM Studio, …). Requires a base URL and model.
contextifly.llm.provider.markdownDescription: Advanced: which LLM provider to use for analysis. Choose `default` to use the backend's own key, or pick a provider and supply your own key in `#contextifly.llm.apiKey#`. The model must support image input.

contextifly.llm.apiKey.markdownDescription: Advanced: your own API key for the selected `#contextifly.llm.provider#`. Sent to the backend with each upload and never stored server-side. Leave blank to use the backend's default key.

contextifly.llm.model.markdownDescription: Advanced: model id for the selected provider (e.g. `gemini-2.0-flash`, `gpt-4o`, `claude-3-5-sonnet-latest`). Required for `openai-compatible`; leave blank elsewhere for the provider's default. Must be a vision-capable model.

contextifly.llm.baseUrl.markdownDescription: Advanced: base URL of an OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g. `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`). Only used when `#contextifly.llm.provider#` is `openai-compatible`.
